persona.title: Persona
persona.prompt: Whose journey is this? Name the protagonist, their situation and goal.
examples: Maria, 38 — Operations Director at a mid-market logistics firm; needs to cut weekly reporting from 6 hours to 1
Jin, first-time SaaS buyer evaluating CRMs for a 12-person sales team
Anya, returning customer who churned 3 months ago and is reconsidering

needs.title: Customer Needs
needs.prompt: What does the customer need to get done at this stage of the journey?
examples: Discovery: trustworthy comparison of options without vendor sales pressure
Onboarding: get a real report out within day one — no "hello world" demo data
Retention: a way to share monthly value with their boss, in their words

key-moment.title: Key Moment
key-moment.prompt: If you took a snapshot of this moment, what would be in frame? Where, what, who, on which channel?
examples: Friday 4pm, laptop on kitchen counter, comparing 3 vendor sites in tabs while kids watch TV
Slack DM to a peer: "Have you used X? Worth it?" — answer arrives 2 days later
Login screen on Monday morning — sees the wrong workspace and nearly closes the tab

satisfaction.title: Customer Satisfaction
satisfaction.prompt: How does the customer feel at this moment — happy, neutral, or frustrated?
examples: Frustrated — pricing page is opaque, has to email sales just to get a number
Neutral — onboarding finished but the value still feels theoretical
Happy — first weekly report saved 4 hours; sent screenshot to teammates
